name: Home
title: Home
description: Homepage that surfaces featured devotionals, sermons, and upcoming Sunday services, provides quick navigation to key ministry areas, and offers a global search across devotionals and sermons.

core_features: Load and display featured devotionals, featured sermons, and upcoming Sunday services using getHomepageHighlights.
Optionally load an extended list of upcoming Sunday services using getUpcomingSundayServices when the user requests more services from the Home page.
Allow users to save or remove featured devotionals from their personal reading list using saveDevotionalToReadingList and removeDevotionalFromReadingList.
Allow users to add or remove featured sermons from their Watch Later list using addSermonToWatchLater and removeSermonFromWatchLater.
Provide a global search bar that queries devotionals and sermons using searchSiteContent, shows quick results, and links to full result pages.
Highlight specific Home sections (devotionals, sermons, services) based on the URL parameter highlight, including when users arrive from other pages such as prayer.html or give.html.
Provide quick navigation links from Home to Devotionals, Sermons, Plans, Sunday Services, Groups, Prayer, Give, and Store pages.
user_workflows:
Discover and read a featured devotional:
1. User navigates to index.html (optionally with ?highlight=devotionals).
2. Page controller calls getHomepageHighlights and populates featured_devotionals.
3. If highlight=devotionals, the page scrolls/focuses the featured devotionals section.
4. User scans the devotional cards and clicks a card title or body.
5. Browser navigates to devotional_detail.html?id={devotional_id of the clicked card}.

Save or remove a featured devotional from the reading list:
1. User is on index.html and sees featured devotionals loaded from getHomepageHighlights.
2. Each devotional card shows a control whose initial state is derived from is_saved_to_reading_list.
3. User clicks the save/remove control:
   - If the devotional is not saved, the component calls saveDevotionalToReadingList(devotionalId).
   - If it is already saved, the component calls removeDevotionalFromReadingList(devotionalId).
4. On a successful response, the button state and any displayed saved-count indicator are updated without reloading the page.
5. If the call fails, an inline error message is shown and the button state remains unchanged.

Discover and watch a featured sermon, and manage Watch Later:
1. User navigates to index.html (optionally with ?highlight=sermons).
2. Page controller calls getHomepageHighlights and populates featured_sermons.
3. If highlight=sermons, the page scrolls/focuses the featured sermons section.
4. User clicks a sermon thumbnail or title.
5. Browser navigates to sermon_detail.html?id={sermon_id}.
6. Alternatively, the user clicks the Watch Later toggle on a sermon card:
   - If not in watch later, call addSermonToWatchLater(sermonId).
   - If already in watch later, call removeSermonFromWatchLater(sermonId).
7. On success, the is_in_watch_later visual state updates and any count indicator is updated.

Plan attendance at a Sunday service from Home:
1. User navigates to index.html (optionally with ?highlight=services).
2. Page controller calls getHomepageHighlights and populates upcoming_sunday_services.
3. If highlight=services, the page scrolls/focuses the upcoming services section.
4. User reviews the listed services and identifies one with status indicating it can be reserved.
5. User clicks the 'Reserve Seats' button on a service card.
6. Browser navigates to service_reservation.html?serviceId={sunday_service_id of the selected service}.
7. If the user wants to see more services from Home, they click a 'View more services' control, which calls getUpcomingSundayServices(fromDate, maxCount) and extends the list.

Use the global search from Home:
1. User focuses the global search bar on index.html.
2. User types a search term.
3. After the user stops typing for a short interval or submits the form, the component calls searchSiteContent(query, maxResultsPerType).
4. The search suggestions panel displays devotional and sermon matches separately.
5. User options:
   - Click a devotional suggestion to navigate to devotional_detail.html?id={Devotional.id}.
   - Click a sermon suggestion to navigate to sermon_detail.html?id={Sermon.id}.
   - Click 'See all devotional results' to navigate to devotionals.html?query={current search term}.
   - Click 'See all sermon results' to navigate to sermons.html?query={current search term}.
6. If query is cleared, the suggestions panel hides and no searchSiteContent call is made until a new query is provided.

Arrive from another page with a section highlight:
1. User is on prayer.html, give.html, sunday_services.html, groups.html, or plans.html.
2. User clicks a navigation element that links to index.html with a highlight parameter (e.g., index.html?highlight=services).
3. On load, the Home page controller reads the URL parameter highlight.
4. The controller calls getHomepageHighlights and renders all sections normally.
5. Once data is ready, the section-highlight controller scrolls and focuses the specified section (devotionals, sermons, or services) and updates internal state to record the current highlighted section.
6. If the user navigates within the page to another section using internal links, the highlight parameter in the URL is updated via pushState so that reloading the page preserves the chosen section.
interactions: On page load, the page controller parses the highlight URL parameter (if any) and calls getHomepageHighlights to load featured_devotionals, featured_sermons, and upcoming_sunday_services.

When the user moves focus into the global search input and types a non-empty query, the global search component triggers searchSiteContent with the current query and a configured maxResultsPerType after a brief delay, then updates the suggestions list.

Submitting the search form (e.g., pressing Enter) uses searchSiteContent if suggestions are not yet loaded, then shows suggestions; the page does not navigate away automatically to preserve the global search context.
Clicking 'See all devotional results' in the search suggestions navigates the browser to devotionals.html?query={current search term}.
Clicking 'See all sermon results' in the search suggestions navigates the browser to sermons.html?query={current search term}.
Clicking a featured devotional card (outside the save/remove control) navigates to devotional_detail.html?id={devotional_id}.

Clicking the save/remove control on a devotional card calls saveDevotionalToReadingList or removeDevotionalFromReadingList based on is_saved_to_reading_list, updates button state, and reflects the total_saved_count reported by the interface where displayed.
Clicking a featured sermon card (thumbnail or title, but not the Watch Later toggle) navigates to sermon_detail.html?id={sermon_id}.

Clicking the Watch Later toggle on a sermon card calls addSermonToWatchLater or removeSermonFromWatchLater based on is_in_watch_later, updates the icon state, and reflects total_watch_later_count where displayed.
Clicking the 'Reserve Seats' button on an upcoming Sunday service card navigates to service_reservation.html?serviceId={sunday_service_id}.
If the user clicks a 'View more services' control, the component calls getUpcomingSundayServices(fromDate, maxCount) and appends or replaces the services list with the returned services array.

Clicking quick navigation links (Devotionals, Sermons, Plans, Sunday Services, Groups, Prayer, Give, Store) navigates directly to the respective pages without calling additional interfaces from the Home page.

When the user clicks in-page links that change the highlighted section (e.g., internal shortcut to devotionals/sermons/services on Home), the section-highlight controller updates the URL's highlight parameter using history APIs and scrolls to the corresponding section.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary external state for the Home page is managed via the URL parameter highlight and through data returned by the assigned interfaces. When index.html loads, the page controller reads window.location.search and looks for a highlight parameter. Accepted values are aligned with section identifiers (e.g., 'devotionals', 'sermons', 'services'); any other value is ignored. This parameter does not alter which data is requested; getHomepageHighlights is always called to retrieve featured_devotionals, featured_sermons, and upcoming_sunday_services. Once data is available, the section-highlight controller uses the highlight value to determine which section should receive initial focus and scroll positioning. Internal navigation within the Home page that conceptually changes the active section (for example, a shortcut link at the top of the page that moves from devotionals to services) updates the highlight parameter in the URL using the browser's history API without reloading the page, ensuring that refreshes and bookmarks preserve the chosen section. No persistent client-side storage such as localStorage or cookies is used; all persistent data resides in backend models and is accessed via the assigned interfaces. The global search component maintains transient state for the current query and the most recent searchSiteContent results only in memory. It does not encode the search term into the Home page URL; instead, when a user chooses to see full results, the search term is passed as a query parameter named query to devotionals.html or sermons.html. The reading list and Watch Later states for items on Home are derived from the is_saved_to_reading_list and is_in_watch_later fields returned by getHomepageHighlights, and are updated in-memory in response to saveDevotionalToReadingList, removeDevotionalFromReadingList, addSermonToWatchLater, and removeSermonFromWatchLater responses. These operations do not modify the URL. The optional extended services list state is maintained in-memory within the upcoming-services component and is updated only when getUpcomingSundayServices is called; this state is not reflected in URL parameters.

components.functionality: Controls initial data loading and section focus on the Home page. On load, reads the highlight URL parameter, calls getHomepageHighlights once to retrieve featured_devotionals, featured_sermons, and upcoming_sunday_services, and distributes this data to child components. After data is available, signals the section-highlight-controller to focus the appropriate section based on the highlight parameter.
event_handlers: onLoad: parse URL parameters, particularly highlight, and invoke getHomepageHighlights().
onHomepageHighlightsLoaded: receive the getHomepageHighlights response object and update page-level state slices for featured_devotionals, featured_sermons, and upcoming_sunday_services.

onURLChange: optional handler to respond to browser back/forward navigation; re-evaluates highlight and re-runs section highlighting without re-calling getHomepageHighlights unless the page is fully reloaded.

components.functionality: Manages which Home page section (devotionals, sermons, or services) is considered active or highlighted based on the highlight URL parameter. After the content is rendered, it scrolls the relevant section into view, moves keyboard focus to an accessible heading or anchor in that section, and can update the highlight parameter when the user navigates between sections within the page.
event_handlers: onSectionsRendered: checks the current highlight value and scrolls/focuses the corresponding section element if it exists.

onInternalSectionLinkClick: when a user clicks an internal link (e.g., a shortcut to the services section), updates the URL's highlight parameter via history.pushState and then scrolls to the section without a full page reload.
onPopState: when the user uses the browser back/forward controls and highlight changes, the controller updates focus and scroll to match the new highlight value.

components.functionality: Provides a single search input on the Home page for searching devotionals and sermons. Manages the query string entered by the user, invokes searchSiteContent, and passes search results to the search-suggestions-panel. The form does not immediately navigate away; navigation only occurs when the user selects a result or a 'see all results' action.
event_handlers: onInputChange: updates the local query state and, after a short debounce interval and if the query is non-empty, calls searchSiteContent(query, maxResultsPerType).
onSubmit: if suggestions are not already loaded for the current query, calls searchSiteContent; then opens or focuses the search-suggestions-panel instead of navigating.
onClearQuery: clears the query input and instructs the search-suggestions-panel to hide or reset its results.
onSearchResultsReceived: receives the devotionals and sermons arrays from searchSiteContent and forwards them to search-suggestions-panel for display.

components.functionality: Displays immediate search results for devotionals and sermons under the global search form. Groups results by content type (devotionals and sermons) and provides navigation to detail pages or full result pages for each type.
event_handlers: onResultClickDevotional: when the user clicks a devotional search result, navigates to devotional_detail.html?id={Devotional.id} using the id from the bound Devotional object.
onResultClickSermon: when the user clicks a sermon search result, navigates to sermon_detail.html?id={Sermon.id}.
onClickSeeAllDevotionalResults: navigates to devotionals.html?query={current search term}.
onClickSeeAllSermonResults: navigates to sermons.html?query={current search term}.
onHidePanel: hides the panel when the query is cleared or when focus moves away from the search context according to defined blur rules.

components.functionality: Renders the list of featured devotionals on the Home page using the featured_devotionals array returned from getHomepageHighlights. Each item is presented as a card with basic devotional metadata and a control to save or remove it from the user's reading list.
event_handlers: onDevotionalCardClick: when the user clicks within a card (excluding the save/remove control), navigates to devotional_detail.html?id={devotional_id}.
onSaveToggleClick: delegates to devotional-reading-list-toggle for the selected devotional; the card updates its saved state based on the toggle's callback.
onDataUpdate: when homepage-initializer updates the featured_devotionals array (for example after a refresh), re-renders the list and propagates is_saved_to_reading_list state to each card.

components.functionality: For each featured devotional card, provides a button or toggle that reflects whether the devotional is currently in the user's reading list (using is_saved_to_reading_list) and allows adding or removing it via the appropriate interface.
event_handlers: onClick: if the devotional's is_saved_to_reading_list is false, call saveDevotionalToReadingList(devotionalId). If true, call removeDevotionalFromReadingList(devotionalId).

onSaveDevotionalToReadingListSuccess: when saveDevotionalToReadingList returns success, update the devotional's local is_saved_to_reading_list state to true and optionally update a displayed total_saved_count from the response.
onRemoveDevotionalFromReadingListSuccess: when removeDevotionalFromReadingList returns success, update local is_saved_to_reading_list state to false and update any displayed total_saved_count.
onActionFailure: display an inline error message associated with this devotional card if either interface call fails; do not change the toggle’s visual state in this case.

components.functionality: Renders the list of featured sermons on the Home page using the featured_sermons array returned from getHomepageHighlights. Each item is presented as a card with sermon metadata and a control to add or remove it from the Watch Later list.
event_handlers: onSermonCardClick: when the user clicks within a sermon card (excluding the Watch Later control), navigates to sermon_detail.html?id={sermon_id}.
onWatchLaterToggleClick: delegates to sermon-watch-later-toggle for the selected sermon; the card updates its is_in_watch_later visual state based on the toggle’s callback.
onDataUpdate: when homepage-initializer updates the featured_sermons array, re-renders the list and propagates is_in_watch_later state to each card.

components.functionality: For each featured sermon card, provides a button or toggle that reflects whether the sermon is currently in the user's Watch Later list (using is_in_watch_later) and allows adding or removing it using the related interfaces.
event_handlers: onClick: if the sermon's is_in_watch_later is false, call addSermonToWatchLater(sermonId). If true, call removeSermonFromWatchLater(sermonId).

onAddSermonToWatchLaterSuccess: when addSermonToWatchLater returns success, update the sermon's local is_in_watch_later state to true and update any displayed total_watch_later_count from the response.
onRemoveSermonFromWatchLaterSuccess: when removeSermonFromWatchLater returns success, update the sermon's local is_in_watch_later state to false and update any displayed total_watch_later_count.
onActionFailure: display an inline error message associated with this sermon card if either interface call fails; do not change the toggle’s visual state in this case.

components.functionality: Displays upcoming Sunday services on the Home page. Initially uses the upcoming_sunday_services array from getHomepageHighlights. Can optionally load a more complete schedule via getUpcomingSundayServices when the user requests more services.
event_handlers: onInitialDataBind: renders service cards using upcoming_sunday_services from getHomepageHighlights, highlighting the one flagged as is_next_upcoming when applicable.
onReserveSeatsClick: when the user clicks the 'Reserve Seats' button for a service with a reservable status, navigates to service_reservation.html?serviceId={sunday_service_id}.

onViewMoreServicesClick: calls getUpcomingSundayServices(fromDate, maxCount) where fromDate is chosen based on the current date or existing services; merges or replaces the displayed list with the returned services array.
onGetUpcomingSundayServicesSuccess: updates the list of services displayed in this section and, if next_upcoming_service_id is provided, optionally scrolls or marks that service as primary.
onGetUpcomingSundayServicesFailure: shows an inline error message in the services section while preserving any already-rendered services from getHomepageHighlights.

components.functionality: Provides quick navigation links on the Home page to key sections of the site: Devotionals, Sermons, Plans, Sunday Services, Groups, Prayer, Give, and Store. These links are simple navigations and do not depend on the Home page interfaces.
event_handlers: onClickDevotionalsLink: navigates to devotionals.html (optionally with additional parameters defined elsewhere in the site).

layout.grid_system: Implicit single central column likely mapped to a standard 12-column grid, with all visible content centered within a full-width hero section.
layout.layout_pattern: Use a single, vertically stacked central column placed on top of a full-width, full-height hero at the top of the page. The hero follows the mockup: top navigation band, centered brand/title, centered scripture, and then the global search in the same central column. Below the hero, all homepage sections (featured devotionals, sermons, upcoming services, quick links) are arranged in a simple top-to-bottom flow, each aligned to the same central column and centered within the page. No sidebars or competing columns; all content is stacked and centered to maintain a devotional, meditative flow.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Use a single, vertically stacked central column placed on top of a full-width, full-height hero at the top of the page. The hero follows the mockup: top navigation band, centered brand/title, centered scripture, and then the global search in the same central column. Below the hero, all homepage sections (featured devotionals, sermons, upcoming services, quick links) are arranged in a simple top-to-bottom flow, each aligned to the same central column and centered within the page. No sidebars or competing columns; all content is stacked and centered to maintain a devotional, meditative flow.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The hero occupies the initial full viewport height, with the top 20–25% dedicated to nav and brand/title, the central 40–50% to scripture and the search form, and the remaining space breathing room with the background image. Below the hero, each section (devotionals, sermons, services, quick links) occupies its own horizontal band with generous vertical spacing between sections (64–96px) and internal padding of 24–32px within each content block. The primary content column spans roughly 70–80% of the viewport width (max-width ~960–1140px) and is centered, with generous side margins to let the background and whitespace frame the content.
layout_strategies.content_density: Maintain low to moderate content density, mirroring the spacious hero. Each section introduces only a small set of items (a short list of devotionals/sermons/services) with generous vertical spacing between rows (16–32px) and clear breathing room above section headings. Avoid dense grids; prefer stacked, easy-to-scan vertical lists and lightly framed cards with ample padding (16–24px). The search and suggestion area remains visually clean, with whitespace separating it from the hero scripture and the next content section.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye starts at the top navigation and brand in the hero, then moves down to the scripture, then to the global search form at the visual center of the hero. When active, the search suggestions panel becomes the immediate focal area just below the search field. As the visitor scrolls, the flow continues down the central column: first featured devotionals (primary reading content), then featured sermons (media focus), then upcoming services (planning and attendance), and finally quick links (utility navigation) near the bottom. Section headings and subtle highlight states guided by the state controller reinforce which section is currently in focus.

layout.overall_description: The homepage opens on a full-bleed hero with a warm sunset photograph stretching edge to edge. At the very top lies a thin, centered navigation strip and brand mark in an elegant serif, lightly separated from the rest of the hero by padding rather than hard lines. Below it, still within the upper quarter of the hero, the ministry name and a subtle cross icon sit centered in large, reverent type. In the middle of the hero, over the darker portion of the image, a centered scripture passage creates a devotional focal point. Immediately beneath the scripture, still anchored to the same central vertical axis, a wide global search form spans about two-thirds of the page width, with plenty of air around it. When the search is engaged, a softly framed suggestions panel unfolds directly below the search, hovering over the lower portion of the hero. Scrolling down, the hero gives way to a sequence of calm, full-width horizontal bands, each containing a centered column: first a featured devotionals band, then featured sermons, then upcoming services, and finally a quick links band leading into the footer. Each section is separated by generous vertical space, with section titles centered or subtly top-aligned within the column and content presented in simple vertical lists or lightly carded blocks. The overall effect is a single, serene vertical journey: from reverent introduction, through focused search and highlighted spiritual content, to practical navigation, all unified by a classic, slightly traditional typographic system and open, contemplative spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component defines the full-page canvas: it establishes the full-width, full-height hero at the top, with the background image stretching edge to edge and up to at least one viewport height. Within it, a centered content column (max-width around 1040px, roughly 70–80% of viewport width) is horizontally centered and used for all primary elements: top navigation, brand/title, scripture, and the global search form. Below the hero, the initializer continues the same central column down the page, stacking each major section (featured devotionals, featured sermons, upcoming services, quick links) with 64–96px of vertical space between them. All subsequent components are positioned relative to this core column, ensuring no sidebars or offset grids—only one central vertical axis of content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Acts as the root layout container; non-scrolling relative to the viewport other than standard page scroll. It anchors the hero background and the central content column but does not itself appear as a visible element.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component operates invisibly within the vertical stack of sections. It conceptually tracks which section (devotionals, sermons, services, quick links) is currently nearest the center of the viewport and may subtly enhance that section’s visual state (e.g., slightly deeper heading color, gentle underline on the section title, or a faint glow on its top border). It does not take up any explicit space; instead, it modulates styling for the sections already placed in the central column beneath the hero.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual and non-spatial; it rides along with scroll behavior, updating styles but not changing the physical layout or occupying dedicated space.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The global search form is centered within the hero, positioned below the scripture block along the same central vertical axis. It sits roughly in the middle third of the viewport height: with about 24–32px of vertical space separating it from the scripture above and at least 64px of breathing room before the bottom of the hero. The input field spans approximately 60–70% of the viewport width on desktop (capped at about 640–720px), with balanced left and right margins so it feels anchored to the center. Above or within the field, a subtle label or placeholder references searching devotionals and sermons without clutter. The form’s height is modest (e.g., 48–56px total), and it rests directly over the darker region of the background image to preserve contrast, effectively becoming the interactive focal point of the hero. On smaller screens, it scales to about 90% width while remaining centered.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the hero; it scrolls away with the hero content. It acts as the anchor for the suggestions panel, which appears immediately below it when active.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The search suggestions panel is tightly coupled to the search form’s position. When active, it appears directly beneath the global search form, perfectly aligning its left and right edges with the search input so the two form a single visual column. Its width matches the search form (around 60–70% of the viewport, max-width ~640–720px), and it extends downward as a vertically stacked list of suggestions, each item separated by 8–16px. The panel floats visually above the hero background with a soft, slightly translucent backing or a subtle card-style container with 16–24px internal padding. The bottom of the panel remains within the hero’s vertical bounds whenever possible; if there are many suggestions, the panel becomes internally scrollable rather than pushing the rest of the page downward, to preserve the hero’s overall proportions and breathing room.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Positioned relative to the global search form, effectively ‘attached’ to its bottom edge. It may be absolutely or fixed-positioned within the hero container so that it overlays the hero image without shifting adjacent sections, and it appears/disappears dynamically based on search interaction.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This section is the first major band immediately below the hero. It starts after a generous gap of about 80px from the bottom edge of the hero, maintaining the open, devotional feel. The section sits inside the same central column (max-width ~1040px) and is horizontally centered. At the top of the section, a heading such as “Featured Devotionals” appears left-aligned within the column but still centered as a block on the page, with 8–16px of space below it. Below the heading, a short descriptive line (if used) and/or the list of devotionals is stacked vertically. The devotionals themselves appear as 2–4 vertically stacked cards, each spanning about 100% of the column width, with 24–32px vertical spacing between cards. Each card includes 16–24px internal padding, subtle borders or shadows, and comfortable line-height for reading. The top and bottom of the section include 32–48px of padding to separate it from adjacent sections.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, scrolls with the rest of the page. It maintains consistent alignment with the central column and does not introduce multi-column layouts; any thumbnail or metadata stays within the single-column card format.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This action button is visually associated with the featured devotionals heading area. It is placed in the same horizontal row as the “Featured Devotionals” title, aligned to the right edge of the central column while the heading sits on the left, creating a subtle two-end alignment within the single main column. There is 8–16px of horizontal spacing between the heading and the button so they don’t feel cramped. The button itself is compact but prominent enough—perhaps pill-shaped or softly rounded—with 12–16px of horizontal padding and 8–12px vertical padding. It sits flush with or slightly below the baseline of the heading text, and a small gap (16–24px) separates this row from the devotional cards below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the featured devotionals section. It does not float or stick; it remains anchored to the heading row and scrolls away naturally as the user moves down the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This section appears directly beneath the featured devotionals, separated by 64–96px of vertical space. It mirrors the devotionals section’s layout to create rhythm and consistency: same central column width, same left-aligned heading within the centered column, and a similar heading row structure. The heading (e.g., “Featured Sermons”) sits at the top with 24–32px of padding above and 8–16px below. Underneath, the sermons are presented in a vertical list of 2–4 items. Each sermon entry spans nearly the full column width and can optionally feature a thumbnail or duration indicator, but these remain integrated within a single-column card layout, with 16–24px padding and 24–32px vertical spacing between cards. Emphasis is on clarity and easy scanning, not dense media grids.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the vertical flow, keeping alignment locked to the central column. It scrolls naturally; no sticky behavior is applied to this section.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This action button pairs with the featured sermons section heading in the same way the devotional toggle does. It is placed on the same horizontal line as the “Featured Sermons” heading, aligned to the right edge of the column, with the heading on the left. The spacing and proportions are consistent: 8–16px separation between heading and button, 12–16px horizontal padding inside the button, and 8–12px vertical padding. Its visual treatment is slightly less bold than primary buttons, but clearly interactive. A small vertical gap (16–24px) below this heading row separates it from the list of sermon items so the controls and content don’t visually blur together.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, anchored to the top of the featured sermons section. It scrolls off-screen with the section and has no independent sticky or floating behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The upcoming services section follows the featured sermons, again separated by roughly 64–96px of vertical space to maintain the open rhythm. It uses the same central column alignment and width. A clear heading (e.g., “Upcoming Sunday Services”) appears at the top, left-aligned within the centered column, with 24–32px padding above to visually mark a new section. Underneath, service entries are listed vertically—likely fewer items (e.g., 2–3 next services). Each entry spans the column width as a concise card or row with service date, time, and location/details, using 16–24px internal padding and 16–24px vertical spacing between items. The section itself may feel slightly more compact than devotionals/sermons but still preserves comfortable line spacing and breathing room above and below (32–48px) so it doesn’t feel utility-heavy.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static in the vertical flow, following the same single-column layout and scrolling behavior as the other sections.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This section is the final content band before the footer, positioned after another 64–96px of vertical space below the upcoming services section. It may sit on a slightly toned background (a very soft tint) to gently signal a transition toward footer/utility content, while still feeling part of the main page. Within the same centered column, a modest heading like “Quick Links” is placed at the top, optionally centered or left-aligned depending on the overall style, with 16–24px of padding above. Below, a vertical stack of key navigation links (e.g., ‘All Devotionals’, ‘All Sermons’, ‘Prayer Requests’, ‘Give’) is presented. Each link is a simple line item, left-aligned within the column, with 12–16px vertical spacing between them, and generous left/right padding (16–24px) if they are contained in subtle list rows. The section has 32–48px of padding at the top and bottom, creating a calm landing zone that leads naturally into the site footer.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static at the bottom of the main content stack. It scrolls normally and does not stick or float; it serves as a gentle bridge to the footer rather than a persistent navigation element.

name: Devotionals
title: Devotionals
description: Search, filter, sort, and browse devotionals with the ability to save items to a personal reading list. Page state is fully controlled by URL parameters so it can be shared and restored.

core_features: Initialize available filter options (topics, reading time ranges, date ranges) using getDevotionalFilterOptions.
Search and filter devotionals by keyword, topic, reading time, and date range using searchDevotionals.
Sort devotional results (e.g., by date or reading time) via the searchDevotionals sort_by parameter.
Paginate devotional results using the searchDevotionals page and page_size parameters.
Display a list of devotionals with title, excerpt, tags, reading time, date, image, and saved/not-saved state.
Save a devotional to the user’s reading list using saveDevotionalToReadingList from the list view.
Remove a devotional from the user’s reading list using removeDevotionalFromReadingList from the list view.
Navigate from a devotional in the list to its devotional_detail.html page.
Navigate from this page to reading_list.html via a dedicated control.
Restore search and filter state from URL parameters (query, topic, maxReadingTime, dateRange, page, sortBy) on load and after navigation.
user_workflows:
Workflow: Initial page load and state restoration
1. User opens devotionals.html directly, from navigation, or via a link from another page.
2. The page reads URL parameters (query, topic, maxReadingTime, dateRange, page, sortBy), if present.
3. The page calls getDevotionalFilterOptions to load available topic_tags, reading_time_options, and date_range_presets.
4. The filter components initialize their selected values from the URL parameters where applicable.
5. The page calls searchDevotionals with:
   - query: value from URL param query (or an empty value if absent),
   - filters: derived from topic, maxReadingTime, dateRange URL parameters,
   - sort_by: from sortBy URL parameter (or an implementation-defined default when absent),
   - page: from page URL parameter (or an implementation-defined default when absent),
   - page_size: an implementation-defined default.
6. The page renders the devotional list, pagination, and sort controls based on the searchDevotionals response.
7. Each devotional item shows whether it is currently saved to the reading list using the is_saved_to_reading_list field.

Workflow: Searching by keyword
1. User types a keyword into the search input on the devotionals page.
2. User submits the search form.
3. The search-form component updates the URL parameter query to the entered value, resets page to the first page, and preserves existing filter parameters (topic, maxReadingTime, dateRange, sortBy).
4. The page triggers a new call to searchDevotionals with the updated query and current filters/sort.
5. The results list updates to show devotionals matching the new keyword search.

Workflow: Filtering by topic, reading time, and date range
1. User opens or interacts with the filter sidebar.
2. The filter component displays available options loaded from getDevotionalFilterOptions.
3. User selects or changes one or more filters (topic tag, maximum reading time, date range preset).
4. On each filter change (or on an explicit apply action, depending on implementation), the filter component updates the corresponding URL parameters (topic, maxReadingTime, dateRange). It also resets the page parameter to the first page while preserving query and sortBy.
5. The page calls searchDevotionals with updated filters and the preserved query and sort order.
6. The results list refreshes to reflect the selected filters.

Workflow: Sorting results
1. User opens the sort control (e.g., a dropdown) that reflects the current sortBy URL parameter.
2. User selects a different sort option.
3. The sort control updates the URL parameter sortBy, resets page to the first page, and preserves query, topic, maxReadingTime, and dateRange.
4. The page calls searchDevotionals with the new sort_by value and existing query and filters.
5. The results list reorders according to the selected sort.

Workflow: Paginating through results
1. User scrolls to the pagination controls.
2. User clicks a page number, next/previous, or another pagination control.
3. The pagination component updates the URL parameter page to the chosen page, preserving query, topic, maxReadingTime, dateRange, and sortBy.
4. The page calls searchDevotionals with the new page value (and the existing filters, query, and sort).
5. The results list updates to show devotionals for the selected page.

Workflow: Saving a devotional to the reading list
1. In the devotional results list, each item shows its saved state (e.g., via a toggle/button) based on is_saved_to_reading_list from searchDevotionals.
2. For an unsaved devotional, user clicks the save action control on that item.
3. The item-level component calls saveDevotionalToReadingList with the devotional’s devotional_id.
4. On success, the component updates that item’s saved state locally and, if desired, updates any reading list count indicator using the total_saved_count value.
5. No URL parameters are changed by this action; search/filter state is preserved.
6. Optionally, if the next call to searchDevotionals occurs (for example, due to subsequent filter changes), the refreshed search results reuse the is_saved_to_reading_list field to remain consistent.

Workflow: Removing a devotional from the reading list
1. In the devotional results list, a saved item shows its saved state.
2. User clicks the unsave/remove action control on that item.
3. The item-level component calls removeDevotionalFromReadingList with the devotional’s devotional_id.
4. On success, the component updates that item’s saved state locally and optionally updates any reading list count indicator using total_saved_count.
5. URL parameters are unchanged; search/filter state is preserved.
6. If a later searchDevotionals call is made (e.g., due to filters or pagination), the results will again reflect the up-to-date saved status via is_saved_to_reading_list.

Workflow: Opening a devotional detail and returning to the list
1. From the results list, user clicks on a devotional’s title or image.
2. The list component navigates to devotional_detail.html with the selected devotional’s ID as a URL parameter (e.g., devotional_detail.html?id=DEVOTIONAL_ID).
3. The devotional detail page may include a "Back to Devotionals" navigation element that links back to devotionals.html with query, topic, maxReadingTime, dateRange, page, and sortBy parameters set to the state that should be restored.
4. When the user follows that back link to devotionals.html, this page again reads the URL parameters and calls getDevotionalFilterOptions (if needed) and searchDevotionals with those parameters to recreate the previous list view.

Workflow: Viewing the full reading list
1. From the devotionals page, user clicks a "View Reading List" control.
2. The page navigates to reading_list.html (without additional required parameters, per outgoing_connections).
3. The reading list page handles its own loading and state; when the user returns from reading_list.html to devotionals.html, any URL parameters provided in that link will govern the restored devotional list state.
interactions: Entering text in the search input and submitting the form triggers a URL update (query, page) and a new searchDevotionals call.
Changing the topic filter selection updates the topic URL parameter, resets page to the first page, and triggers searchDevotionals.
Changing the maximum reading time filter updates the maxReadingTime URL parameter, resets page to the first page, and triggers searchDevotionals.
Changing the date range preset filter updates the dateRange URL parameter, resets page to the first page, and triggers searchDevotionals.
Clearing all filters resets topic, maxReadingTime, and dateRange URL parameters, resets page to the first page, and triggers searchDevotionals with only the keyword (if any) and sortBy.
Changing the sort control updates the sortBy URL parameter, resets page to the first page, and calls searchDevotionals with the new sort_by.
Clicking pagination controls updates the page URL parameter and calls searchDevotionals with the selected page.
Clicking a devotional title or image navigates to devotional_detail.html with the devotional_id passed as the id URL parameter.
Clicking the save action on a devotional item calls saveDevotionalToReadingList and, on success, updates that item’s saved state without changing URL parameters.
Clicking the remove/unsave action on a devotional item calls removeDevotionalFromReadingList and, on success, updates that item’s saved state without changing URL parameters.
Clicking the "View Reading List" control navigates to reading_list.html.
Reloading or sharing the page URL causes the same search and filters to be restored because the page reads query, topic, maxReadingTime, dateRange, page, and sortBy from the URL on load.
page_functionality.state_logic: The Devotionals page uses URL parameters as the single source of truth for its search and filter state. On every page load, the page reads the following parameters: query (keyword string), topic (selected topic tag), maxReadingTime (maximum reading time in minutes), dateRange (selected date range preset), page (current page number), and sortBy (sort order identifier). These values are used to initialize all UI controls and to construct the arguments for searchDevotionals. Whenever the user changes the search term, filters, sort, or pagination, the corresponding URL parameters are updated via client-side navigation (e.g., modifying the query string and triggering routing) rather than storing state in localStorage or another client-side store. After each such update, the page calls searchDevotionals with arguments derived from the current URL. The searchDevotionals parameters map as follows: query is taken directly from the query URL parameter (or an empty value if absent), filters is an object derived from topic, maxReadingTime, and dateRange (only including filters that are present), sort_by is taken from the sortBy URL parameter (or an implementation-defined default if absent), page is taken from the page URL parameter (or an implementation-defined default if absent), and page_size is an implementation-defined value that can be kept constant or exposed by additional UI controls if desired. The getDevotionalFilterOptions interface is called independently of the URL state to retrieve available filter options; however, the chosen option values are synchronized with topic, maxReadingTime, and dateRange URL parameters. Reading list actions do not affect URL parameters; instead, they rely on the is_saved_to_reading_list field returned by searchDevotionals along with the responses from saveDevotionalToReadingList and removeDevotionalFromReadingList to determine and update saved states within the current view.

components.functionality: Coordinates initial page load, URL parameter parsing, retrieval of filter options, and execution of the initial searchDevotionals call. Also re-executes searchDevotionals whenever URL parameters relevant to search or filters change.
Provides a text input for keyword-based devotional search and a submit action. Reads the current query URL parameter to initialize its field and updates it when the user submits a new search.

Displays filter controls for topic tags, maximum reading time, and date range presets. Initializes its available options using the data from getDevotionalFilterOptions and its selected values using topic, maxReadingTime, and dateRange URL parameters. When filters change, it updates those URL parameters and triggers a new searchDevotionals call via the controller.

Provides a control (e.g., dropdown or segmented control) for choosing the sort order of devotional results. Reflects the current sortBy URL parameter and updates it when the user changes the selection. Triggers the controller to re-run searchDevotionals.

Renders the list of devotionals returned from searchDevotionals. Each item shows title, excerpt, topic_tags, reading_time_minutes, date_published, image_url, and saved state based on is_saved_to_reading_list. Delegates item-level interactions (open detail, save/unsave) to the devotional list item component.

Represents a single devotional entry within the results list. Handles click on title or image to navigate to devotional_detail.html with id set to the devotional_id. Displays a save/unsave control whose state reflects is_saved_to_reading_list and uses saveDevotionalToReadingList or removeDevotionalFromReadingList accordingly.

Displays pagination based on searchDevotionals.total_count, searchDevotionals.page, and searchDevotionals.page_size. Handles user navigation to different pages by updating the page URL parameter and triggering searchDevotionals with the new page value.

Optionally shows an indicator of how many devotionals are saved to the reading list, based on total_saved_count returned from saveDevotionalToReadingList or removeDevotionalFromReadingList. Does not directly modify URL parameters or call searchDevotionals.
Provides a control that navigates the user to reading_list.html when clicked. May be used in conjunction with the reading list indicator component.
Shows and hides a loading state while getDevotionalFilterOptions or searchDevotionals requests are in progress. Ensures the user understands when data is being refreshed after URL or filter changes.

Displays a message and optionally a control to clear filters when searchDevotionals returns no items. It reacts to the current query and filters (derived from URL parameters) but does not change them unless the user explicitly chooses to clear or adjust filters.

Centralizes reading and writing of URL parameters for this page. It exposes methods to get the current query, topic, maxReadingTime, dateRange, page, and sortBy values, and to update them in response to UI events. When updating, it ensures that only the relevant parameter is changed (and that page is reset when appropriate) while preserving the rest of the state. It notifies the controller when a relevant URL change occurs so that searchDevotionals can be re-run.

layout.grid_system: Implicit single central column likely mapped to a standard 12-column grid, with all visible content centered within a full-width hero section.
layout.layout_pattern: The page keeps the mockup’s single, centered column as the spine of the layout. At the top, a full-width hero section with a photographic background contains a slim navigation strip (including the reading list link/indicator) and a large, centered page title. Directly beneath the title sits a short scripture line or devotional tagline, followed by a centered search form. Below the hero, the main content area continues the vertical stack: a compact control row (filter trigger + sort control + reading list status), then the results list, and finally pagination. All primary interactive elements are aligned on this central axis; only the filter panel temporarily breaks the single-column feel by sliding in as an off-canvas sidebar when invoked.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page keeps the mockup’s single, centered column as the spine of the layout. At the top, a full-width hero section with a photographic background contains a slim navigation strip (including the reading list link/indicator) and a large, centered page title. Directly beneath the title sits a short scripture line or devotional tagline, followed by a centered search form. Below the hero, the main content area continues the vertical stack: a compact control row (filter trigger + sort control + reading list status), then the results list, and finally pagination. All primary interactive elements are aligned on this central axis; only the filter panel temporarily breaks the single-column feel by sliding in as an off-canvas sidebar when invoked.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The hero occupies roughly 65–75% of the initial viewport height, echoing the reverent hero of the mockup. The top 20–25% of this hero is reserved for navigation and the Devotionals heading; the central 40–50% holds the scripture line and search form over the darker part of the image for legibility; the remainder is mostly unobstructed image. Below the hero, a white or softly tinted content area spans the full width, but the functional content is constrained to a centered column at about 70–80% of the page width (max ~960–1040px). Vertical gaps of 64–96px separate major sections (hero → controls → list → pagination) to maintain openness.
layout_strategies.content_density: The hero area is intentionally sparse—just nav, title, a short scripture, and the search field—with generous breathing room between each element (24–48px) and ample margins from the viewport edges. The main content area has moderate density: controls are compact but not cramped, results items are card-like with 24–32px padding and 24–32px gaps between them, and pagination is given its own open band near the bottom. There are no busy sidebars in the default view; filters are hidden until requested to preserve the contemplative feel.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Visual flow follows a strong top-to-bottom path. The eye first meets the top nav strip and the reading list link/indicator at the upper edge, then drops to the prominent Devotionals heading in serif type and any cross/brand mark. From there it moves down to the centered scripture or tagline, then to the search form, which is the main interaction focal point in the hero. Scrolling down, attention passes to the filter/sort row, then to the first devotional card in the results list. The list leads the eye down to pagination controls. When the filter sidebar is open, it intentionally overlays from the right as a temporary focus, but the underlying central column still anchors perception.

layout.overall_description: The Devotionals page presents as a calm, hero-led experience that transitions into a focused reading and discovery area. At the very top, a full-bleed sunset or worship-themed photograph fills the viewport width, with a translucent gradient darkening the lower middle to make text readable. Along the top edge of this hero, a slim horizontal navigation strip stretches from left to right, with the site logo and primary nav (outside the scope here) on the left and a subtle Reading List link with a small circular count indicator aligned to the right. Centered below this strip, occupying the upper quarter of the hero, the word “Devotionals” appears in an elegant, all-caps serif, accompanied by a small golden cross or emblem. Dropping into the central band of the hero, a short scripture line or devotional tagline sits in neutral sans-serif, centered and spanning roughly half to two-thirds of the page width. Just beneath it, the search form is aligned to the same central axis, a wide, pill-shaped input stretching across about 60–70% of the width, with abundant whitespace above and below. The lower portion of the hero image remains largely unobstructed, letting the silhouetted shapes and sunburst breathe. Below this, the page transitions into a soft, light background where a centered content column holds a compact row of controls—filter access, sort options, and a small reading list status hint—followed by a vertical stack of devotional cards. Each card occupies the full width of the central column with generous padding, separated by comfortable vertical gaps, creating a sense of calm browsing. At the bottom, centered pagination maintains the same measured rhythm. When filters are opened, a semi-opaque overlay dims the page and a right-side panel glides in, but the underlying structure remains that single, reverent column guiding the user down the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This controller component has no visible UI. It sits conceptually at the top of the Devotionals page hierarchy, just beneath the page container, managing data fetching for the hero search, filters, and results. It occupies no explicit width or height and does not alter the centered visual layout; instead, it orchestrates when the loading and empty states replace the results list within the main central column.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual, zero-size element in normal document flow; it influences which visual components are mounted but does not render visible space.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The search form is positioned in the lower half of the hero section, centered horizontally on the page and aligned with the vertical centerline of the Devotionals heading above. It spans approximately 60–70% of the viewport width on desktop, capped at around 640–720px, with equal left and right margins ensuring it never touches the edges. There is generous vertical spacing: roughly 24–32px above it from the scripture/tagline text and 32–48px below it before the empty breathing zone at the bottom of the hero image. The search input itself can be a single, pill-shaped field with a submit button integrated on the right, maintaining the spacious, reverent feel with 16–24px of internal padding.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the hero’s vertical flow; remains centered and responsive, shrinking to a higher percentage width (e.g., 80–90%) on smaller screens while maintaining its central alignment.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: In its default, closed state, the filter sidebar contributes a small, textual entry point within the main controls row directly beneath the hero. This entry point appears as a subtle “Filters” pill or icon-button aligned near the left side of the central column, in the same horizontal band as the sort control, with about 16–24px of separation from neighboring elements. When activated, the component manifests as a right-hand slide-in panel that overlays the page content: it anchors to the right edge of the viewport, occupying roughly 320–360px in width and the full viewport height. A semi-transparent, slightly darkened backdrop covers the rest of the page behind it, but the user can still perceive the central column of content beneath. Inside the panel, filters are stacked vertically with 24–32px padding on all sides, echoing the airy spacing of the main content. The top of the panel aligns roughly with the top of the viewport, feeling like an extension of the page rather than a separate modal.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Hidden by default, then transitions into a fixed-position overlay attached to the right edge of the viewport when open. It sits above other content layers with a backdrop overlay and can scroll independently if its content exceeds the viewport height.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The sort control is placed immediately below the hero section, inside the main content area’s central column. It appears within a slim control bar that spans about 70–80% of the page width, matching the width of the results list. Horizontally, the sort dropdown or segmented control is aligned towards the right side of this bar, visually balancing the filter entry point which sits towards the left. The bar itself sits approximately 64px below the bottom edge of the hero, with 16–24px of vertical padding to keep it from feeling cramped. The entire controls row (filter access on the left, sort control on the right, and possibly a subtle results count centered or left) remains visually light so that it doesn’t overpower the content, using minimal borders and plenty of whitespace.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static element in the normal document flow, locked to the top of the main content area. On narrow viewports it can collapse into a stacked layout, with sort control centered beneath the filter trigger while maintaining comfortable vertical spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The results list occupies the heart of the main content area, directly beneath the controls row. It is constrained to a centered column that spans approximately 70–80% of the viewport width on desktop, up to around 960–1040px. There is a vertical gap of about 32px between the controls row and the first devotional item, maintaining the page’s openness. Each child list item is stacked vertically, one per row, with 24–32px of vertical spacing in between. The side margins within the content area remain generous, ensuring plenty of empty space to the left and right of the list, echoing the hero’s framed central composition. When in loading or empty states, this entire zone is taken over by the loading or empty-state components, centered both horizontally and vertically within the approximate height of several cards.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block-level container in the central column; it stretches vertically as more items load and scrolls with the page, maintaining its centered alignment on all screen sizes.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each devotionals list item appears as a card-like block within the results list, spanning the full width of the central content column but never touching the viewport edges. Cards have a soft, rectangular shape with slightly rounded corners, and internal padding of 24–32px on all sides to maintain the airy, devotional feel. The primary title (devotional name) is aligned left within the card, but the card itself is centered within the page’s main axis, preserving the overall single-column composition. Below the title, supporting details such as date, scripture reference, and a short excerpt stack vertically with 8–16px gaps. A subtle action row (e.g., a “Save to Reading List” control and perhaps a subtle icon indicating saved state) aligns to the bottom right of the card, with enough whitespace around it to feel unhurried. Vertically, each card is separated from the next by 24–32px of blank space, so the list feels like a series of distinct contemplative entries rather than a dense feed.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static children of the results list, expanding vertically with content. On smaller screens, the card’s internal layout collapses gracefully into a simple top-to-bottom stack while preserving its padding and separation.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The pagination controls are positioned below the results list, centered on the same vertical axis as the hero title and search form. There is a generous vertical gap—roughly 48–64px—between the bottom of the last list item and the pagination row, giving a sense of closure to each page of results. The pagination bar itself is compact and spans about 40–60% of the central column’s width, with page numbers and arrows arranged horizontally and centered. There is ample whitespace above and below the controls to keep them from feeling like a dense footer; the surrounding area remains mostly empty, giving users a clear visual cue that they’ve reached the end of the current batch of devotionals.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static element at the bottom of the main content flow; it moves naturally down as more results are rendered and aligns centrally on all screen sizes.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The reading list indicator appears as a small, circular badge displaying the count of saved devotionals. It is visually paired with the reading list link in the top navigation strip of the hero. Positioned in the top right corner of the hero’s nav row, the badge sits either immediately to the right of the link text or slightly overlapping its top-right corner, depending on the chosen style. The badge is small—around 16–20px in diameter—with enough contrast to be seen against the hero background, but not so prominent that it competes with the central Devotionals heading. There is at least 16px of margin from the top and right edges of the viewport, ensuring it feels contained within the nav strip and consistent with the minimalist, reverent layout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Effectively inline with the reading list link in the hero’s top nav; it scrolls away with the hero as users move down the page and does not remain sticky.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The reading list link is integrated into the skinny top navigation row of the hero, aligned to the right side to balance the logo or site name on the left. It appears as a simple text link or a minimal text-plus-icon treatment set in the same elegant serif or a matching, understated sans-serif, maintaining consistency with the nav style from the mockup. Horizontally, it is positioned near the right edge of the central content area but still within 24–32px of the viewport edge, leaving a comfortable margin. Vertically, it sits within the top 10–15% of the hero height, sharing the horizontal line with other nav items and with 16–24px of padding above and below to give the nav strip breathing room. The reading list indicator badge is visually attached to this link, making it clear that the count belongs to this destination.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Part of the hero’s static top nav; it scrolls away with the hero and does not remain fixed once the user scrolls into the main content area.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The loading state occupies the same central zone as the results list. When active, it replaces the list items with a centered loading indicator (such as a spinner or a set of shimmering placeholders) positioned roughly halfway down the space that would normally be occupied by several cards. This indicator is aligned on the page’s central vertical axis and constrained to about 40–60% of the central column width, ensuring it feels grounded in the same single-column structure. The area around the loader is intentionally sparse, with at least 32–48px of empty space on all sides, maintaining the serene, uncluttered feel even while loading. Optional dimmed card placeholders can stack vertically, still honoring the 24–32px spacing rhythm of the real list items.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally rendered in place of the results list area; it does not overlay the hero or controls but sits inside the normal flow of the central column content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The empty state also lives within the main results list zone and appears when no devotionals match the current search and filters. It is presented as a centered message block spanning about 50–60% of the central column width, with a soft card treatment or simple text-only layout. The block is vertically offset so that its main message sits slightly below the top of the results area—roughly where the first few cards would normally appear—keeping the composition balanced and not too close to the controls row above. Inside, a short, empathetic headline and supporting text are stacked with 8–16px spacing, possibly accompanied by a simple, line-based icon. There is 32–48px of padding around the content, and at least 48px of clear space between this block and both the controls row above and the pagination area (which may be hidden in this state), preserving the open, contemplative feel of the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally replaces the results list items inside the central column; it scrolls naturally with the rest of the content and does not overlay other sections.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This URL state synchronization component has no visual appearance. It conceptually sits alongside the load controller near the root of the Devotionals page, listening to changes in search terms, filters, sort selection, pagination, and reading list interactions, and reflecting those in the URL. It does not take up any width or height and does not influence the spatial arrangement of visible components, but ensures that the user’s view of the central column (filters applied, page selected) can be restored and shared.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual, zero-size element in normal document flow; it reacts to and updates state without affecting layout or occupying space.

name: Devotional Detail
title: Devotional
description: Displays a single devotional reading with full content, scripture references, metadata, reading-list save state, and navigation controls.

core_features: Load and display a single devotional using getDevotionalDetail based on the id URL parameter.
Show devotional metadata such as title, publication date, reading time, topic tags, scripture references, author, and image when available.
Indicate whether the devotional is saved to the user’s reading list and allow toggling that state using saveDevotionalToReadingList and removeDevotionalFromReadingList.
Provide a 'Back to Devotionals' link that returns to devotionals.html with the original search and filter state restored via URL parameters.
Provide navigation to the previous and next devotionals using IDs returned by getDevotionalDetail.
Allow the user to share the devotional (e.g., via native share or by copying the URL) and print the devotional content.
Offer a follow-up option to go to the reading_list.html page after successfully saving a devotional.
user_workflows:
View a devotional (from Devotionals list):
1. User clicks a devotional in devotionals.html; link opens devotional_detail.html with id and optional returnQuery, returnTopic, returnMaxReadingTime, returnDateRange, returnPage.
2. devotional_detail.html reads the id parameter and calls getDevotionalDetail(devotionalId=id).
3. Page displays the devotional content, metadata, and scripture references.
4. Page shows whether the devotional is already saved (based on is_saved_to_reading_list) and displays appropriate save/remove controls.
5. User can read content, navigate to previous/next devotionals, or go back to devotionals.html with the original filters restored.

View a devotional (direct link/external share):
1. User opens devotional_detail.html with only id in the URL (no return* parameters).
2. Page calls getDevotionalDetail(devotionalId=id).
3. Devotional content and metadata are displayed.
4. 'Back to Devotionals' falls back to a generic devotionals.html link (without query, topic, reading time, date range, or page parameters).

Save a devotional to the reading list:
1. On load, getDevotionalDetail returns is_saved_to_reading_list = false.
2. User clicks the save control.
3. Page calls saveDevotionalToReadingList(devotionalId=id).
4. On success, the component updates local page state to reflect that the devotional is now saved, updates any saved-count indicator using total_saved_count, and shows a confirmation message using message.
5. A prompt offers a 'View Reading List' action. If the user clicks it, the browser navigates to reading_list.html.

Remove a devotional from the reading list:
1. On load, getDevotionalDetail returns is_saved_to_reading_list = true.
2. User clicks the remove control.
3. Page calls removeDevotionalFromReadingList(devotionalId=id).
4. On success, the component updates local page state to mark the devotional as not saved, updates any saved-count indicator using total_saved_count, and shows a confirmation or info message using message.

Navigate to previous or next devotional:
1. After getDevotionalDetail resolves, the page receives previous_devotional_id and next_devotional_id (one or both may be absent).
2. If previous_devotional_id exists, the Previous control is enabled; if next_devotional_id exists, the Next control is enabled.
3. User clicks Previous: the page navigates to devotional_detail.html with id set to previous_devotional_id and preserves any existing return* parameters in the URL.
4. User clicks Next: the page navigates to devotional_detail.html with id set to next_devotional_id and preserves any existing return* parameters.
5. On each navigation, getDevotionalDetail is called again for the new id and the page updates its content and save state.

Return to Devotionals list:
1. User clicks the 'Back to Devotionals' link.
2. If returnQuery, returnTopic, returnMaxReadingTime, returnDateRange, and/or returnPage are present in the current URL, the page constructs a link to devotionals.html with the corresponding parameters mapped to query, topic, maxReadingTime, dateRange, and page.
3. Browser navigates to devotionals.html, where the list page can restore the previous filter and pagination state.

Share or print a devotional:
1. User clicks a Share action.
2. The page constructs a share payload using the current page URL (including id and any return* parameters) and devotional title, then invokes the browser’s share mechanism or copies the URL to the clipboard.
3. User clicks a Print action.
4. The page triggers the browser print dialog so the user can print the devotional content.
interactions: Initial load: On page load, read id (required) and any return* parameters from the URL. If id is present, call getDevotionalDetail(devotionalId=id). If id is missing or getDevotionalDetail fails, display an error state with an option to navigate back to devotionals.html.

Save devotional: User clicks the save control when is_saved_to_reading_list is false. Trigger saveDevotionalToReadingList(devotionalId=id). While waiting, show a loading state on the button and disable multiple clicks. On success, toggle UI to the 'saved' state, update any saved count indicator, and show a confirmation. On failure, show an error message and revert button state.

Remove devotional: User clicks the remove control when is_saved_to_reading_list is true. Trigger removeDevotionalFromReadingList(devotionalId=id). While waiting, show a loading state and disable multiple clicks. On success, toggle UI to the 'not saved' state, update any saved count indicator, and show a confirmation or neutral message. On failure, show an error message and revert button state.

Back to Devotionals click: User clicks 'Back to Devotionals'. The component builds a URL to devotionals.html, mapping returnQuery → query, returnTopic → topic, returnMaxReadingTime → maxReadingTime, returnDateRange → dateRange, returnPage → page, only including parameters that exist in the current URL. Browser navigates to the constructed URL.

Previous devotional click: User clicks the Previous control (if previous_devotional_id exists). The component reads all current return* parameters, navigates to devotional_detail.html with id set to previous_devotional_id and the same return* parameters appended. New page instance loads and calls getDevotionalDetail for the new id.
Next devotional click: User clicks the Next control (if next_devotional_id exists). Behavior mirrors the Previous control, using next_devotional_id.
View Reading List click: After a successful saveDevotionalToReadingList call, user may click 'View Reading List'. Browser navigates directly to reading_list.html (no additional parameters required).

Share click: User clicks Share. The component retrieves the current window URL and devotional title, then either invokes a native share API (if available) or copies the URL to the clipboard and shows a brief confirmation message.
Print click: User clicks Print. The component calls the browser’s print functionality so the current devotional content is formatted for printing.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary page state is driven by URL parameters and the response from getDevotionalDetail. The id parameter is required; on every load or navigation within devotional_detail.html, the page reads id and calls getDevotionalDetail(devotionalId=id). The response provides devotional data, is_saved_to_reading_list, previous_devotional_id, and next_devotional_id, which are stored in in-memory state for this page instance and bound to components. The returnQuery, returnTopic, returnMaxReadingTime, returnDateRange, and returnPage parameters, if present, are treated as opaque values used only to construct the 'Back to Devotionals' URL and to be forwarded unchanged when navigating to previous or next devotionals. When the user navigates to previous or next devotionals, the page builds a new URL for devotional_detail.html, setting id to the chosen adjacent devotional ID and preserving all existing return* parameters. This ensures that 'Back to Devotionals' always returns the user to the same filtered/devotionals list context they originally came from. The save state in the UI is always based on the latest is_saved_to_reading_list value from getDevotionalDetail, overridden by optimistic updates in response to saveDevotionalToReadingList and removeDevotionalFromReadingList results within the same page session. No reading-list or devotional state is stored in localStorage; only the URL and live API responses control the page state.

components.functionality: Initializes the page by reading URL parameters, calling getDevotionalDetail with the id parameter, and managing loading and error states for the devotional data.

Displays the devotional title and key metadata (such as publication date, reading time, topic tags, scripture references summary, and author when available) and provides the 'Back to Devotionals' navigation link.
Renders the full devotional content text, scripture reference list, and optional image in a readable layout for on-screen reading and printing.

Shows the current saved/unsaved state of the devotional, provides a button to save or remove it from the reading list, and reflects changes from saveDevotionalToReadingList and removeDevotionalFromReadingList.
After a successful save to the reading list, shows a confirmation message and optionally a call-to-action to navigate to the reading_list.html page.

Provides Previous and Next devotional controls using IDs returned from getDevotionalDetail and navigates to the corresponding devotional_detail.html view while preserving any existing return* URL parameters.

Provides share and print actions for the devotional: constructs a share payload using the current URL and devotional title, triggers the browser share mechanism or copies the link, and opens the browser print dialog.
Displays appropriate error messages when the id URL parameter is missing or when getDevotionalDetail fails, and offers navigation options back to devotionals.html or another safe page.
Updates the browser document title and optional meta information based on the loaded devotional title to give context in browser tabs and shared links.

layout.grid_system: Implicit single central column likely mapped to a standard 12-column grid, with all visible content centered within a full-width hero section.
layout.layout_pattern: All devotional detail content is arranged in a single, centered vertical column that sits on top of a full-width hero-style header image at the top of the page. The devotional header behaves like the mockup’s hero: it fills the top of the page with a photographic background and centered typography (title + key scripture). Below the hero, the main devotional text flows straight down the center, followed by secondary utilities (share/print, reading list prompt if needed) and finally adjacent navigation at the bottom. Overlays like loader and prompts sit centered above everything, dimming the page but not breaking the basic top-to-bottom, single-column flow.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: All devotional detail content is arranged in a single, centered vertical column that sits on top of a full-width hero-style header image at the top of the page. The devotional header behaves like the mockup’s hero: it fills the top of the page with a photographic background and centered typography (title + key scripture). Below the hero, the main devotional text flows straight down the center, followed by secondary utilities (share/print, reading list prompt if needed) and finally adjacent navigation at the bottom. Overlays like loader and prompts sit centered above everything, dimming the page but not breaking the basic top-to-bottom, single-column flow.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The hero-style devotional header occupies roughly the upper 40–50% of the initial viewport on larger screens, mirroring the mockup’s use of space: upper portion for title/metadata, central portion for scripture excerpt. The devotional content viewer then uses the next 40–50% of vertical space as the primary reading area. Utility actions (share/print, adjacent navigation) and any prompts occupy the remaining space with generous vertical margins. Horizontally, all core content (header text, content, controls) sits within a centered column roughly 60–70% of the page width (max ~720–800px), while the background image and page canvas extend full width to frame it.
layout_strategies.content_density: The layout keeps a low, contemplative content density in the hero: just the devotional title, a minimal set of metadata, and a central scripture reference, all with generous breathing room. The main content area allows slightly higher density (paragraphs of text) but maintains ample line spacing and 24–32px padding around the text column to preserve the meditative feel. Section breaks—between hero and content, between content and adjacent navigation—use 64–96px vertical spacing to echo the openness of the mockup. Utility controls are compact and visually quiet, grouped together so they do not clutter the primary reading column.
